title: Neocortical and Hippocampal Theta Oscillations Track Audiovisual Integration and Replay of Speech Memories
description: This dataset contains raw MEG recordings, anatomical T1-weighted MRI scans, and behavioral data from an audiovisual speech memory study. Participants performed a three-phase task involving encoding of audiovisual speech movies with synchrony judgments, a distractor task, and retrieval of speech memories cued by speaker faces. The study investigates how neocortical and hippocampal theta oscillations support audiovisual integration and memory replay of speech information.
methods_description: MEG data were collected during a single session per participant using continuous recording split into five runs. The experimental paradigm consisted of repeated blocks with three successive tasks: (1) encoding phase with audiovisual speech movies (5 s) and synchrony detection responses, (2) distractor phase with odd/even number judgments (20 trials), and (3) retrieval phase with visual speaker cues (5 s) followed by two-alternative forced choice between matching and non-matching auditory speech stimuli. Anatomical T1-weighted MRI scans were acquired for some participants.
